codeReview.summary: The codex/claude active-state routing tests were made environment-aware as intended, but the same commit also altered a different launch test in a way that reintroduces environment-dependent failure when Claude is installed.

issues.description: `test_launchCLI_rejectsConcurrentLaunchSameProfile` now executes two launches sequentially (`await` then `await`, lines 935-936) and asserts at least one failure (`XCTAssertTrue(hasAlreadyInProgress || !outcome1.success || !outcome2.success)`). When `/opt/homebrew/bin/claude` is installed, `launchCLI` can succeed for both calls (process launch returns success immediately in `CLILaunchInvoker.launchCLI`, OpenBurnBarCore/Sources/OpenBurnBarCore/SwitcherCLILAunchService.swift:572-575), making this assertion fail and causing environment-dependent app-test behavior.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The feature's verification step in mission shared state points to the wrong XCTest class, which can produce a false-green targeted run with zero executed tests.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: /Users/dewclaw/.factory/missions/76dfbf36-62ab-43ad-899f-b8b8f9e47932/features.json:1555 uses `-only-testing:"OpenBurnBarTests/SwitcherCrossFlowTests/test_activeStateRouting_cliLaunch_rapidSwitchABC_usesFinalCommittedProfile"`, but the test is defined in `SwitcherCLILaunchServiceTests` (AgentLensTests/Active/SwitcherCLILaunchTests.swift:1254). Transcript skeleton for worker session 580791e8-65db-4e5b-9a84-d1af6a8d3597 records an initial run with "Executed 0 tests" before correcting the selector.

summary: Reviewed the assigned feature's handoff, transcript skeleton, skill context, and commit diff. Result is fail due to one blocking regression: a modified same-profile CLI launch test now depends on Claude installation state and can fail on machines where Claude is installed.
